description: VP Engineering with 18 years of experience, scaled team 45 to 180, drove $120M ARR product, reduced attrition significantly, worked at Netflix/Amazon/Microsoft
resumeText:
Michael Thompson
michael.thompson@email.com | (206) 555-0913 | linkedin.com/in/mthompson-eng | Seattle, WA

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Vice President of Engineering with 18 years of progressive leadership experience building and scaling high-performing engineering organizations at Netflix, Amazon, and Microsoft. Proven ability to align engineering strategy with business objectives, delivering complex platform initiatives that drive measurable revenue and operational outcomes. Expert at hiring, developing, and retaining senior technical talent in competitive markets.

EXPERIENCE

Vice President of Engineering — StreamlineHQ, Seattle, WA
March 2018 – Present
- Scaled engineering organization from 45 to 180 engineers across 12 product squads, maintaining delivery velocity and quality throughout hyper-growth phase
- Owned technical strategy and execution for flagship $120M ARR product; led full platform re-architecture from monolith to event-driven microservices, enabling 3x scale and 99.95% uptime SLA
- Reduced voluntary engineering attrition from 25% to 8% over 3 years by implementing structured career laddering, mentorship programs, and quarterly skip-level engagement reviews
- Partnered with CPO to define 3-year technology roadmap; secured $18M capital allocation for platform modernization initiative approved by board
- Built out data platform (Snowflake, dbt, Airflow) enabling real-time analytics for product and revenue teams; reduced time-to-insight from 3 weeks to 2 hours
- Established Site Reliability Engineering (SRE) function from scratch; reduced MTTR by 68% and eliminated two major customer-facing outages per quarter on average
- Instituted quarterly architecture review board and internal engineering standards program adopted by all 12 squads

Director of Engineering — Netflix, Los Gatos, CA
February 2014 – February 2018
- Led 4 engineering teams (60+ engineers) responsible for Netflix's content delivery and streaming pipeline serving 150M+ subscribers globally
- Drove re-architecture of CDN selection algorithm, improving stream quality scores by 18% and reducing buffering events by 23% across all regions
- Established and scaled Netflix's chaos engineering practice within the streaming org; contributed internal tooling later open-sourced as part of the Netflix OSS ecosystem
- Partnered with finance and legal to deliver GDPR compliance program for engineering, on time and within $2.2M budget

Senior Engineering Manager — Amazon, Seattle, WA
April 2010 – January 2014
- Managed 3 teams (28 engineers) building AWS billing and metering infrastructure processing $4B+ in annual cloud spend
- Delivered AWS Cost Explorer MVP in 14 months from concept to GA, which became a top-10 AWS feature by customer satisfaction within 6 months of launch
- Grew team headcount by 12 engineers in 18 months; maintained team health score above 85th percentile for entire tenure

Software Development Engineer → Lead SDE — Microsoft, Redmond, WA
July 2006 – March 2010
- Progressed from IC to technical lead on the Azure Storage team during the foundational build-out of Microsoft's cloud platform
- Designed and implemented table storage replication protocol handling petabyte-scale data with strong consistency guarantees

EDUCATION
M.B.A. — The Wharton School, University of Pennsylvania, 2009
M.S. Computer Science — Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT), 2006
B.S. Computer Science — University of Michigan, 2004 (summa cum laude)

SKILLS & COMPETENCIES
Leadership: Organizational design, headcount planning, executive communication, board presentations
Technical: Distributed systems, microservices, cloud infrastructure (AWS, Azure, GCP), data platforms
Processes: OKRs, Agile at scale (SAFe), SRE practices, incident management, architecture governance
Tools: JIRA, Confluence, Looker, Snowflake, Terraform

SPEAKING & RECOGNITION
- Keynote Speaker: "Engineering Culture at Scale" — QCon San Francisco 2022
- Panelist: "Building Resilient Platforms" — AWS re:Invent 2021
- Forbes Next 1000: Honoree, 2023
